source.sourcePath: raw/sources/DHF (인허가)/02_ADD (HAD1417MCW, HAD1717MCW)/05_설계개발 검증 및 유효성 확인/12_Biocompatibility/13-02 MSDS Data (Doc.No.SHAEC2001131107).PDF

sourceSummary.oneLine: SGS test report for polycarbonate films and sheets, model G11, documenting RoHS and selected chemical test results.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Provides third-party laboratory evidence that the submitted sample complied with RoHS Directive (EU) 2015/863 limits for listed restricted substances.
keyPoints: Sample identified as polycarbonate films and sheets with Model No. G11.
Testing period was 16 Jan 2020 to 23 Jan 2020.
Cadmium, Lead, Mercury, Hexavalent chromium, PBBs, PBDEs and listed phthalates complied with RoHS Directive (EU) 2015/863 limits.
Test methods referenced IEC 62321 series methods with ICP-OES, UV-Vis and GC-MS analysis.
Additional tests reported ND results for SCCP, PFOS and PAHs.

entities.name: SUZHOU OMAY OPTICAL MATERIALS CO., LTD
entities.evidence: SUZHOU OMAY OPTICAL MATERIALS CO.,LTD . NO.158-30,Huashan road,SND,Suzhou,China

entities.name: SGS-CSTC Standards Technical Services (Shanghai) Co., Ltd.
entities.evidence: Signed for and on behalf of SGS-CSTC Standards Technical Services (Shanghai) Co., Ltd..

entities.name: polycarbonate films and sheets
entities.evidence: The following sample(s) was/were submitted and identified on behalf of the clients as : polycarbonate films and sheets.

entities.name: RoHS Directive (EU) 2015/863
entities.evidence: comply with the limits as set by RoHS Directive (EU) 2015/863 amending Annex II to Directive 2011/65/EU.

concepts.name: RoHS restricted substances
concepts.evidence: Cadmium, Lead, Mercury, Hexavalent chromium, Polybrominated biphenyls (PBBs), Polybrominated diphenyl ethers (PBDEs) and Phthalates such as Bis(2-ethylhexyl) phthalate (DEHP), Butyl benzyl phthalate (BBP), Dibutyl phthalate (DBP) and Diisobutyl phthalate (DIBP)

concepts.name: Perfluorooctane Sulfonates
concepts.evidence: PFOS refer to Perfluorooctanesulfonic acid and its derivatives

concepts.name: Polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons
concepts.evidence: Polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons (PAHs). Test Method : . With reference to AfPS GS 2014:01 PAK

evidence.claim: The document is Test Report No. SHAEC2001131107 dated 23 Jan 2020.
evidence.evidenceText: Test Report. No. SHAEC2001131107 Date: 23 Jan 2020 . Page 1 of 14.

evidence.claim: The submitted sample was identified as polycarbonate films and sheets.
evidence.evidenceText: The following sample(s) was/were submitted and identified on behalf of the clients as : polycarbonate films and sheets.

evidence.claim: The model number listed for the sample was G11.
The sample was received on 16 Jan 2020 and tested from 16 Jan 2020 to 23 Jan 2020.
evidence.evidenceText: Date of Sample Received : . 16 Jan 2020. Testing Period : . 16 Jan 2020 - 23 Jan 2020 .

evidence.claim: The performed RoHS tests complied with the limits set by RoHS Directive (EU) 2015/863 amending Annex II to Directive 2011/65/EU.
evidence.evidenceText: Based on the performed tests on submitted sample(s), the results of Cadmium, Lead, Mercury, Hexavalent chromium, Polybrominated biphenyls (PBBs), Polybrominated diphenyl ethers (PBDEs) and Phthalates such as Bis(2-ethylhexyl) phthalate (DEHP), Butyl benzyl phthalate (BBP), Dibutyl phthalate (DBP) and Diisobutyl phthalate (DIBP) comply with the limits as set by RoHS Directive (EU) 2015/863 amending Annex II to Directive 2011/65/EU.

evidence.claim: RoHS testing referenced IEC 62321 series methods and used ICP-OES, UV-Vis and GC-MS analysis.
evidence.evidenceText: With reference to IEC 62321-4:2013+AMD1:2017, IEC62321-5:2013, IEC62321-7-2:2017, IEC 62321-6:2015 and IEC62321-8:2017, analyzed by ICP-OES, UV-Vis and GC-MS.

evidence.claim: Cadmium, Lead, Mercury, Hexavalent Chromium, Sum of PBBs and Sum of PBDEs were reported as not detected for specimen 001.
evidence.evidenceText: Cadmium (Cd) . 100 mg/kg. 2 ND Lead (Pb). 1000 mg/kg. 2 ND Mercury (Hg). 1000 mg/kg. 2 ND Hexavalent Chromium (Cr(VI)) . 1000 mg/kg. 8 ND Sum of PBBs . 1000 mg/kg. - ND Sum of PBDEs . 1000 mg/kg. - ND

evidence.claim: DBP, BBP, DEHP and DIBP were reported as not detected for specimen 001.
evidence.evidenceText: Di-butyl Phthalate (DBP) . 1000 mg/kg. 50 ND Benzyl Butyl Phthalate (BBP) . 1000 mg/kg. 50 ND Di-2-Ethyl Hexyl Phthalate (DEHP) . 1000 mg/kg. 50 ND Diisobutyl Phthalates (DIBP) . 1000 mg/kg. 50 ND

evidence.claim: The report states the maximum permissible limit is quoted from RoHS Directive (EU) 2015/863.
evidence.evidenceText: The maximum permissible limit is quoted from RoHS Directive (EU) 2015/863.

evidence.claim: The report states the IEC 62321 series is equivalent to the EN 62321 series.
evidence.evidenceText: IEC 62321 series is equivalent to EN 62321 series

evidence.claim: SCCP testing referenced US EPA 3550C:2007 and reported SCCP as not detected.
evidence.evidenceText: With reference to US EPA 3550C: 2007, analysis was performed by GC-ECD / GC-NCl-MS . Short-chain Chlorinated Paraffin (SCCP) (C₁₀-C₁₃) . mg/kg. 50 ND

evidence.claim: PFOS testing referenced NPR CEN/TS 15968:2010 and reported PFOS as not detected.
evidence.evidenceText: With reference to NPR CEN/TS 15968:2010, analysis was performed by LC-MS. Perfluorooctane Sulfonates (PFOS)^ . mg/kg. 10. ND.

evidence.claim: PAHs testing referenced AfPS GS 2014:01 PAK and used GC-MS.
evidence.evidenceText: Polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons (PAHs). Test Method : . With reference to AfPS GS 2014:01 PAK, analysis was performed by GC-MS.

evidence.claim: The report is the English version of SHAEC2001131108.
evidence.evidenceText: This report is the English version of SHAEC2001131108 .

reviewFlags.reason: No revision or version number is present in the provided source text.
The source is a third-party SGS test report, not a document issued directly by a regulatory authority.
Source path labels the file as MSDS Data, but the provided text identifies the document as a Test Report.
Many client reference model identifiers are listed, but only Model No. G11 is clearly identified as the model number.
